item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like everything before the second addendum sans some minor quips on the writing itself, but the addendum kinda ruined the tone of the article for me - especially since its purpose feels more to tie-in a canon than as a supplement like one would usually expect. It feels like a story plonked on the end crammed into the format of an incident log, bookended by paragraphs that are probably the worst offenders, especially with the "happily ever after" nature of the latter paragraph. There's nothing wrong with "good endings" for an SCP, but I honestly think it would be better suited written as a separate tale since that seems to be more what you were going for, and just link it at the end of this article - that way you're not trying to do two things at once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd like to see this as a canon someday. The Kephart Corporation seems like it would have fingers in other pies besides serial murder, hotels and meat packaging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the group called SixthStar or SixStar? Both are used seemingly interchangeably.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, having read all four articles, here is what I've gathered.

The Kephart Corporation:

  • They work in black mail
  • They hate celebrities
  • They have multiple memetic triggers at their disposal
  • They seemingly care about the environment
  • They are incredibly capitalistic
  • They are very good at making cheap human clones

And this might just be part of your writing style, but

  • Everything they do is designed to create the plot of a suspense thriller/drama with aspects of comedy and romance slipped in

Am I right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it just me or is this SCP document reminding me of crime dramas like NCIS, and CSI?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Day 3 entry of the 72 hour contest!

The final part of the Kephart Trilogy. This is also loosely connected to Glittering Horizon, which I gotta finish someday.

This is my first exploration log in two years of writing for the site. Yay!

Special thanks to Tobe Hooper, John Woo and Algernon.
